g.text: timegm(&tm) ← NOT mktime() — avoids local timezone offset

text.tspan: = means client cache is still valid

div.p: ≤ (not <) Comparison Semantics st.st_mtime <= ims HTTP dates carry 1-second granularity. A file modified at exactly the same UTC second as the cached timestamp is still considered unmodified — the cache remains valid. Using < instead of <= would force an unnecessary re-transfer every time the timestamps are equal.
What 304 Saves ✓ No open() syscall ✓ No read() loop ✓ No 842-byte body transmitted ✓ stat() latency is the only cost (typically < 10 µs from hot inode cache)
timegm() vs mktime() mktime() applies local timezone → wrong UTC timegm() treats struct tm as UTC → correct Requires -D_GNU_SOURCE on Linux to expose timegm() from <time.h>
